channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Georgia primary ballot selection shows Democratic turnout ahead of Republicans for first time in decades
item.description: In Georgia’s most recent primary election, voters selecting Democratic ballots outnumbered those choosing Republican ballots by a notable margin, marking the largest gap in favor of Democrats in rough...

item.title: Filip Hrgovic defeats Dave Allen in three rounds, lines up potential fight with Moses Itauma
item.description: Croatian heavyweight boxer Filip Hrgovic put on a dominant performance when he stopped Dave Allen inside three rounds at the Eco-Power Stadium in Doncaster on May 16. From the first bell, Hrgovic cont...

item.title: Trump Shares Series of Truth Social Posts Before Scheduled Walter Reed Medical Visit
item.description: President Donald Trump made a rapid series of 19 posts on his Truth Social platform early Tuesday morning before departing for Walter Reed National Military Medical Center for what the White House des...

item.title: Trump’s Proposed $1.8 Billion Fund Sparks Divisions Within Republican Party
item.description: A report published by Raw Story examines growing tensions within the Republican Party following Donald Trump’s proposal for a $1.8 billion fund intended to compensate allies who claim they were unfair...

item.title: Veteran journalist Baldwin Ndaba honoured for fearless reporting and mentorship
item.description: South Africa’s journalism community has paid tribute to veteran political reporter Baldwin Ndaba following his death on 23 May 2026. Colleagues, editors, politicians and former newsroom peers remember...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard issues advisory on possible Chinese rocket debris near Northern Luzon waters
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard through its District North Eastern Luzon (CGDNELZN) has issued a maritime advisory warning seafarers and fishing communities in Northern Luzon about the potential fall of ro...

item.title: Trump reportedly urged Middle Eastern leaders to join the Abraham Accords during Iran war negotiations
item.description: According to reports from Axios and Zeteo, President Donald Trump surprised several Middle Eastern leaders during a phone call held on Saturday while discussions about ending the U.S. conflict with Ir...

item.title: Reform and Restore Britain clash over fears of splitting the right-wing vote in the Makerfield by-election
item.description: The article focuses on growing tensions between Reform UK and Rupert Lowe’s newly formed Restore Britain party ahead of the Makerfield by-election. Reform figures have accused Lowe of placing personal...

item.title: Democratic lawmakers condemn DNC Memorial Day social media post for politicizing fallen service members
item.description: Two Democratic lawmakers, both military veterans, publicly criticized their own party after the Democratic National Committee (DNC) posted a Memorial Day message that referenced U.S. service members k...

item.title: NASA Psyche spacecraft captures Mars images during gravity-assist flyby en route to asteroid mission
item.description: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft, launched in October 2023, recently completed a close flyby of Mars as part of its long journey toward the metallic asteroid Psyche in the asteroid belt. The maneuver was used...

item.title: Report alleges political pressure campaign influenced Trump’s Iran peace negotiations and subsequent military actions
item.description: A report cited by Zeteo claims that President Donald Trump faced significant internal and external political pressure surrounding his administration’s efforts to negotiate a potential peace agreement ...

item.title: Democrats Raise Concerns Over Anonymous Republican-Linked PAC Spending in Democratic Primaries
item.description: A report from Punchbowl News describes growing concerns among Democratic lawmakers about the emergence of anonymous and difficult-to-trace super PACs that are spending heavily in Democratic primary el...

item.title: Novo Nordisk lowers Ozempic costs for uninsured Canadians after generic semaglutide launches
item.description: Novo Nordisk announced that some Canadians will soon be able to purchase brand-name Ozempic at a reduced price following the arrival of generic semaglutide products in Canada. The company said eligibl...

item.title: Jet2 confirms stable summer flight operations despite fuel supply concerns
item.description: Jet2 has issued a reassuring update to passengers regarding concerns about possible summer holiday flight cancellations, stating that it expects its flight schedule to operate normally throughout the ...

item.title: Eni Accelerates Baleine Offshore Expansion Project in Ivory Coast Amid Stronger Oil Prices
item.description: Italian energy company Eni is moving to accelerate the expansion of its offshore Baleine oil and gas project in Ivory Coast, a development estimated to cost approximately $4 billion. The company said ...

item.title: Trump’s Endorsement of Ken Paxton Deepens Divisions Within Texas Republicans
item.description: A growing conflict within the Texas Republican Party has intensified after President Donald Trump endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over longtime Senator John Cornyn in the state’s Republican...

item.title: Opinion Column Argues Trump’s Recent Actions Are Increasing Political and Public Backlash
item.description: This opinion article by Jason Miciak argues that President Donald Trump has entered a politically dangerous phase in which his recent actions are testing the limits of public and institutional toleran...

item.title: Ontario Extends Alcohol Service Hours for FIFA World Cup While B.C. Keeps Existing Rules
item.description: Ontario and British Columbia, the two Canadian provinces hosting matches during the FIFA World Cup 2026, are taking different approaches to alcohol service hours during the tournament. Ontario Premier...

item.title: Driver and two accomplices arrested in Edo over alleged diversion and attempted sale of company truck
item.description: Operatives of the Edo State Security Corps have arrested a driver, Muhammed Magaji, alongside two alleged accomplices, Muhammed Bashir and Abba Abubakar, over claims that they tried to divert and sell...

item.title: Sally Ride: Biography of the First American Woman in Space and NASA Career Highlights
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at Dr. Sally Ride, an American physicist, astronaut, and educator who became the first American woman to travel into space. Born in Los Angeles in 1951, Ride ...
